body:
9.39 MILESTONE: compiled clean (0 errors, 0 warnings). .mq4 sent to files/from-agent/ with manifest.

ITEM 3 -- full SaveSystemState/LoadSystemState reconciliation, traced every FileWrite*/FileRead* call in both functions field-for-field. Found it was actually two distinct bugs, both bigger than the original 'double-write' theory:

(a) A stray FileClose(handle) sat in the MIDDLE of SaveSystemState(), right before the live-adaptive-intelligence section. Everything after it (live adaptive WRs, alignment history, tier state, candle weights, FIX25 fields) was silently written to an already-closed handle and never reached disk. LoadSystemState()'s FileIsEnding() guards masked this by gracefully skipping those sections -- which is exactly why it went undetected. Removed the stray close.

(b) SaveSystemState() was missing a calibTs (calibration timestamp) integer write that LoadSystemState() unconditionally reads for its staleness check -- this shifted every field read after it by 4 bytes for the rest of the file. Added the missing write.

(c) Found while checking (b): there's a SEPARATE function, SealArmedSave(), called once at the exact moment calibration completes (Phase 6 passes) -- and it was writing a DIFFERENT version number (9191, no calibTs, missing the requiredWinRate/P6_AvgRR/calibratedWR fields) to the SAME file LoadSystemState() reads. Since LoadSystemState() only accepts version 9196, any restart happening before the first live SaveSystemState() call after calibration would have its just-completed calibration silently rejected and discarded. Aligned SealArmedSave() to write the identical 9196 format.

(d) Added a FileIsEnding() guard around the FIX25 tail read block (regime TP/SL-by-type, discrimination scores, continuation/reversal/breakout WRs) -- it was the one section with no such guard, so a short file (e.g. one written by SealArmedSave right after calibration, before any live-adaptive data exists) would silently zero those arrays instead of leaving them at safe defaults.

ITEM 6: done, reported last update.

COHERENCE PASS -- resolved what 9.38 identified but flagged as needing the full picture:
- Added GetRegimeStrategy(regime) as one shared, bounds-checked accessor; routed ValidateEdge, ValidateRisk, and CalculateOptimalPositionSizing through it (previously all three indexed regimeStrategies[currentRegime] with zero bounds checking). Falls back to regime 9 (the most defensive config) on an out-of-range value. DetermineOptimalTPSL's own local 0-8 clamp was left alone -- it's shared with other 9-sized arrays in that function and was already safe.
- Fixed the dead SL-adjustment code in DetermineOptimalTPSL: the atrPercentile-based widen/tighten logic was computing against selectedSL before it was ever assigned, then getting silently overwritten. Deferred it to apply after the real tier-selection assignment -- it now actually takes effect.
- Fixed the fabricated R-multiple fallback in OnTick's trade-history processing: instead of assuming +2.5R/-1.0R when entry/risk data looks invalid, it now skips recording that trade's R-multiple entirely rather than feeding a guessed number into the learning system's win-rate/RR stats.
- Fixed the Bayesian belief system: it now carries the previous posterior forward as each new bar's prior (85% retained, 15% reverts toward uniform -- a forgetting factor so genuine regime change still gets through) instead of resetting to a uniform prior and re-deriving from a single bar's evidence every call. Gated to only persist on actual bar transitions so within-bar repeat calls don't feed back into themselves.

NOT changed, flagged for your awareness rather than touched: the correlation-model regime override in DetectCurrentRegime() (milestone 3) still doesn't sync back to g_RegimeState.currentRegime -- left as-is since I genuinely don't know if that's intentional (confirmation-only signal) or unfinished wiring, and didn't want to guess at a behavior change there.

9.39 is ready. Will start the 9.40 thesis document next, as instructed -- that's a substantial writing task on its own, will send it in relay message(s) before writing any 9.40 code.
